name: Code Repository Audit
description: Audits any GitHub repository for security vulnerabilities, outdated dependencies, code quality issues, and contributor health. Generates a structured health score with pre-filled GitHub issue links for one-click remediation.

auditScope.description: What to audit: security vulnerabilities, dependency health, code quality, or everything

fields.name: issues
fields.description: Array of issues with title, severity, description, createIssueUrl, reasoning

fields.name: issueCount
fields.description: Counts by severity: { critical, high, medium, low }

invariants.invariant: PHASE 1: EXPLORE & REASON. Before generating any output, create an artifact 'audit-reasoning.md' documenting: (1) repository structure overview, (2) files examined, (3) raw findings per category, (4) filtering decisions (what was included/excluded and why).
invariants.measurement: Verify 'audit-reasoning.md' artifact exists before any issues are reported in output.

do: Step 1: Create audit-reasoning.md. Step 2: Map repo structure. Step 3: Examine key files. Step 4: Document findings.
dont: Skip the reasoning phase and jump straight to the final JSON output.

invariants.commentary: Forcing a reasoning phase drastically improves audit accuracy and reduces false positives.

invariants.invariant: When scope includes security: scan for OWASP Top 10 categories (injection, auth bypass, XSS, insecure deserialization, etc.), hardcoded secrets/API keys, unsafe dependency patterns, and missing security headers. Report >= {{securityThreshold}} severity.
invariants.measurement: Verify security findings reference specific OWASP categories or CVE IDs where applicable.

do: Flag hardcoded AWS_SECRET_KEY in config.js as Critical
Reference CVE-2024-XXXX for known vulnerability in lodash@4.17.20
dont: Report theoretical vulnerabilities without evidence in the code
Ignore hardcoded secrets because they might be test values

invariants.invariant: When scope includes deps: check for outdated packages (compare against latest registry versions), known CVEs in dependencies, unused dependencies, and lockfile consistency. Cross-reference against npm/PyPI/crates.io registries.
invariants.measurement: For each dependency issue, verify external source was checked (registry version, CVE database).

do: Verify 'react' version in package.json against npm registry latest
Check snyk.io or osv.dev for known vulnerabilities
dont: Assume dependency versions are current without checking external data

invariants.invariant: When scope includes quality: assess test coverage patterns, error handling completeness, code duplication, documentation quality, and contributor activity (bus factor). IGNORE: dist/, node_modules/, vendor/, build/, and auto-generated files.
invariants.measurement: Confirm no issues reference files in dist/, node_modules/, vendor/, or .min.js files.

do: Flag critical path without error handling in src/api/handler.ts
Note bus factor of 1 if only one contributor in past 6 months
dont: Report lint errors in minified or third-party code

invariants.invariant: For each issue, generate a pre-filled GitHub issue URL: https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/issues/new?title=TITLE&body=BODY&labels=LABELS. URL-encode title and body. Group 5+ similar minor issues into single 'Cluster' issues to avoid spam.
invariants.measurement: Each issue must have a valid createIssueUrl. If >5 similar minor issues exist, verify they are grouped.

do: Group 5 missing return types into one 'Fix Missing Return Types in /src' cluster issue
dont: Create 5 separate issues for the same minor style violation

invariants.invariant: Calculate healthScore (0-100) deterministically: Start at 100. Deduct: Critical (-25), High (-10), Medium (-5), Low dependency issue (-2), Quality nit (-1). Floor at 0. Show the calculation in the reasoning artifact.
invariants.measurement: Verify healthScore = max(0, 100 - sum(deductions)). Calculation must appear in audit-reasoning.md.

dont: Assign arbitrary scores like '80 because it looks okay'
